it:95.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:95.3:
"There are these three ways of obtaining sensual pleasures. Which three? Those whose sensual pleasures are already provided, those who delight in creating, those with control over what is created by others.
These are the three ways of obtaining sensual pleasures."
it:95.4: Devas whose pleasures are already provided, those with control, those who delight in creation, and any others enjoying sensual pleasures in this state here or anywhere else, don't go beyond the wandering-on.
it:95.5: Knowing this drawback in sensual pleasures, the wise should abandon all sensual desires, whether human or divine.
it:95.6: Having cut the flow of greed for lovely, alluring forms so hard to transcend, having, without remainder, comprehended suffering, they are, without remainder, totally unbound.
it:95.7: They, the wise, with an attainer-of-wisdom's noble vision, right gnosis, directly knowing the ending of birth, come to no further becoming.
it:95.8: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
